headline: The Dow Jones Industrial Average Changes Forever Today: Google Parent Alphabet Is In, Verizon Is Out, and a Longtime Component Is on Thin Ice
summary: This marks the 54th time since the Dow's inception more than 130 years ago that companies have been added or removed from the index.

content.title: The Dow Jones Industrial Average Changes Forever Today: Google Parent Alphabet Is In, Verizon Is Out, and a Longtime Component Is on Thin Ice
content.summary: This marks the 54th time since the Dow's inception more than 130 years ago that companies have been added or removed from the index.

thumbnail.caption: A New York Stock Exchange floor trader looking up in awe at a computer monitor.

headline: BT and Verizon strike deal to merge international businesses
summary: The joint venture will serve more than 3,000 customers across over 180 countries.

headline: BT Group and Verizon to form joint venture, creating a scaled international connectivity platform for multinational customers
summary: BT Group and Verizon agree to combine their respective international operations in a 50:50 joint venture, creating a new company focused on multinational connectivity.The joint venture will serve more than 3,000 customers across more than 180 countries, representing approx. $4 billion in combined annual revenue.The combination of international networking businesses creates a future-ready, scaled organization underpinned by a new platform designed for the age of cloud and AI.Martijn Blanken is ap

headline: BT and Verizon Combine International Operations in $625 Million Joint Venture
summary: The 50/50 deal creates a new entity with $4 billion in combined revenue serving 3,000 customers across 180 countries.

content.summary: The 50/50 deal creates a new entity with $4 billion in combined revenue serving 3,000 customers across 180 countries.

headline: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason
summary: The shares were up 1.2% in premarket trading.  The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

content.title: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason
content.summary: The shares were up 1.2% in premarket trading.  The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

headline: Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?
summary: Three retirees own the same portfolio. Each holds $1 million in dividend-paying stocks generating roughly $60,000 a year of income. One lives in Florida, one in Pennsylvania, and one in California. By the time the year ends, they will not have the same amount of spending power, even though their brokerage statements look identical. State ... Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?

content.title: Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?

headline: Comcast, Alphabet, Palantir, SpaceX, Microsoft, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Technology stocks were trading higher in premarket trading Monday as the  looked set to finally snap its five-day losing streak.  Semiconductor companies, other artificial-intelligence stocks, and the Magnificent Seven were among those rebounding to start the week.  Microsoft  jumped 2% ahead of the open, while  Amazon Alphabet Nvidia Meta  and  Tesla  were all up around 1%.

headline: Alphabet Just Joined the Dow, Right in the Middle of Its Worst Month in Over a Year
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average added Alphabet today, and the timing is the joke. Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) replaces Verizon (NYSE:VZ) in the 30-stock benchmark on the same day the stock is staggering toward its worst month since February of last year, with the quarter and the month ending tomorrow. headline: Verizon falls 7%, AT&T hits 52-week low as SpaceX and cable rivals converge
summary: Investing.com -- Verizon Communications Inc (NYSE:VZ) is trading 7.6% lower at $43.02 and AT&T Inc (NYSE:T) has touched its 52-week low of $21.29, sliding 5.8% to $21.41, as three separate competitive threats collided on Monday to rattle investor confidence in legacy US telecom carriers. T-Mobile US Inc (NASDAQ:TMUS) is also under significant pressure, trading down 6% at $171.78 and testing its 52-week low of $169.00, making it the worst performer among the three carriers on a one-year basis wit

headline: Victra, Verizon's Largest Exclusive Authorized Retailer, Recognized Among Comparably's 2026 Best Sales Teams
summary: Victra, the largest exclusive authorized Verizon retailer in the U.S., has been named a winner of Comparably's 2026 Best Sales Teams Award. The recognition is based entirely on anonymous employee feedback collected over the past 12 months and reflects the enthusiasm, engagement, and satisfaction of Victra's sales associates across its nationwide retail footprint.

content.title: Victra, Verizon's Largest Exclusive Authorized Retailer, Recognized Among Comparably's 2026 Best Sales Teams

headline: Alphabet debuts in Dow Jones Industrial Average as index tilts toward tech
summary: Alphabet shares climbed on Monday as the Google parent made its debut in the blue-chip Dow Jones ‌Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications, and immediately ranking among its ‌most influential members.  • Its shares rose 3.7% to $350.24, offering one of the biggest boosts ​to the 30-member Dow.  • The company replaced Verizon on the index, S&P Dow Jones Indices said in an announcement on June 23.

headline: 66 With $1.3 Million. Here Are 3 Hidden Gems to Target
summary: At 66 with $1.3 million, the math is straightforward. A 5% blended yield throws off roughly $65,000 a year before Social Security. The cash flow underneath these three blue chips is what matters most for a retiree. I dug into each dividend to see which ones a 66-year-old can actually rely on. headline: Google Parent Alphabet Joins The Dow
summary: The move validates that advertising and AI are considered foundational to businesses. Google's focus is on AI and it is expected to give the Dow more exposure to companies focused in the technology.

headline: Alphabet Joins the Dow Today. Surging AI Demand Is Pushing Shares Higher.
summary: The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.  Alphabet’s move comes amid a broader rebound for the Magnificent Seven group of stocks on Monday.

content.title: Alphabet Joins the Dow Today. Surging AI Demand Is Pushing Shares Higher.

Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.  Alphabet’s move comes amid a broader rebound for the Magnificent Seven group of stocks on Monday.
